title: Prometheus 2.50 Release Notes
script: Welcome to your briefing on Prometheus 2.50 release notes. This release brings several important updates and improvements to the popular monitoring and alerting toolkit. Let's dive into the key highlights. First, this release includes performance improvements to the query engine, with optimizations for range queries and better memory management. The TSDB storage layer has been enhanced with improved compression algorithms, reducing disk usage by up to 15 percent in typical workloads. On the API front, there are new endpoints for better observability of Prometheus itself, including detailed metrics about query performance and resource usage. The web UI has received updates with improved visualization of long-running queries and better error reporting. Security-wise, this release addresses several CVEs and improves authentication handling in federated setups. For operators, there are new configuration options for fine-tuning scrape intervals and timeout behaviors. The release also includes bug fixes for edge cases in service discovery, particularly with Kubernetes environments running at scale. Notable breaking changes include deprecation of some legacy configuration options, so be sure to review your Prometheus configs before upgrading. The development team recommends testing this release in staging environments first, especially for large-scale deployments. Overall, Prometheus 2.50 represents a solid incremental improvement with focus on performance, reliability, and operational excellence. This concludes your briefing on Prometheus 2.50.
